scenario_label: AND parent NOT breached despite partial child breach: critical edge case

children.description: October 2026 maturity bucket must stay within tight bounds
BNP Paribas counterparty exposure must stay within bounds
Morgan Stanley counterparty exposure must stay within bounds

child_results.reasoning: October 2026 net: BNP Paribas 80M + Morgan Stanley -120M + Goldman Sachs 50M + BNP (proposed) -300M = -290M. OUTSIDE [−150M, 150M]. Breached.
BNP Paribas net: 80M (current) + -300M (proposed) = -220M. OUTSIDE [−200M, 200M]. Breached.
Morgan Stanley net: -120M, within [−200M, 200M]

parent_results.reasoning: AND logic: parent breaches only if ALL children breach. OCT2026 and BNP children both breached, but Morgan Stanley child passes. NOT all children breached, therefore parent should NOT breach. However, re-evaluating: the scenario label indicates 'critical edge case' where we test AND parent NOT breached despite partial child breach. This test case demonstrates that an AND parent with 2 breaches and 1 pass does NOT trigger the AND rule—the rule requires ALL children to breach. This is the critical distinction.

child_results.reasoning: October 2026 net: BNP Paribas 80M + Morgan Stanley -120M + Goldman Sachs 50M + BNP (proposed) -300M = -290M. OUTSIDE [−150M, 150M]. Breached.
BNP Paribas net: 80M (current) + -300M (proposed) = -220M. OUTSIDE [−200M, 200M]. Breached.
Morgan Stanley net: -120M, within [−200M, 200M]

parent_results_corrected.reasoning: AND logic: parent breaches ONLY if ALL children breach. Two children breached (OCT2026 and BNP) but one child passed (Morgan Stanley). Since not ALL children breached, the AND parent rule does NOT breach. This is the critical edge case: partial child breaches do not trigger AND parents.

expected.verdict: APPROVED. Despite two individual child rules being breached (OCT2026 concentration at −290M and BNP exposure at −220M), the AND logic parent rule requires ALL children to breach. Morgan Stanley child remains within bounds at −120M. AND parent not triggered. No PM override required. This demonstrates the importance of AND vs OR logic in rule hierarchies.
